title: Build a Professional Login Page
description: Create a production-style login form with validation, loading, error handling, and accessibility. Simulate authentication — no real backend required today.
requirements: Email input with controlled value and onChange handler
Password input with controlled value and validation (minimum 8 characters)
Show/hide password toggle that switches input type between password and text
Remember me checkbox bound to boolean state
Client-side validation before submit with per-field error messages
Submit handler with preventDefault and async simulated login (setTimeout)
Loading state: disabled submit button and loading label while request runs
Error message displayed when simulated login fails
TypeScript interfaces for form data and component props
Responsive layout centered on page for mobile and desktop
acceptanceCriteria: Empty email shows validation error without calling login
Invalid email format shows validation error
Password under 8 characters shows validation error
Successful simulated login clears error and exits loading state
Failed simulated login shows error message and re-enables submit
Show password toggle changes input type without clearing password value
Remember me state updates when checkbox is toggled
Pressing Enter in password field submits the form
npm run build completes without TypeScript errors
hints: Keep validation in a pure validateLoginForm function returning { isValid, errors }.
Use separate state for fieldErrors and global login error.
Simulate API: await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1500)); then check credentials.
Use aria-invalid and aria-describedby on inputs with errors.
Button: type="submit" disabled={isLoading} — loading text replaces label.

bonus.title: Polish
bonus.description: Signals that separate you from tutorial copy-paste.
requirements: Disable submit until email and password are non-empty (in addition to validation on submit)
Add focus management: move focus to error summary on failed login
Extract a reusable InputField component — preview of Day 03

debuggingScenarios.title: Wrong state update
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Checkbox or field appears stuck; React DevTools shows state changed but UI did not
debuggingScenarios.cause: Direct mutation of state object followed by setState with same reference
debuggingScenarios.fix: Use functional updates and spread: setForm(prev => ({ ...prev, rememberMe: checked }))

debuggingScenarios.title: Input not updating
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Cannot type in email or password field
debuggingScenarios.cause: Controlled input missing onChange, or value bound to wrong state variable
debuggingScenarios.fix: Ensure value and onChange both reference the same state slice; remove conflicting defaultValue

debuggingScenarios.title: Wrong event handling
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Page reloads on submit or double submissions occur
debuggingScenarios.cause: Missing preventDefault or submit button not disabled during loading
debuggingScenarios.fix: handleSubmit starts with event.preventDefault(); set isLoading true before async work and disable button
